foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Over 90% of acute cholecystitis is caused by gallstone impaction in the cystic duct; understanding stone types and pathogenesis is foundational

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Knowledge of Calot's triangle, cystic artery variants, and the critical view of safety is essential for cholecystectomy and avoiding bile duct injury

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Concomitant CBD stones occur in 10-15% of cholecystitis cases and must be investigated with LFTs and imaging before surgery

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Ascending cholangitis can complicate cholecystitis when stones migrate into the CBD causing biliary sepsis (Charcot's triad/Reynolds' pentad)

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Chronic cholecystitis is a risk factor for gallbladder carcinoma; incidental cancer may be discovered at cholecystectomy

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Bile duct injury is the most feared complication of cholecystectomy, especially in inflamed Calot's triangle with obscured anatomy

integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Acute Cholecystitis across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
